name: In the current study the efficacy and toxicity of the combination of GEM and VNB in elderly patients with advanced NSCLC or those with some contraindication to receiving cisplatin were assessed .

name: METHODS : Forty - nine patients with advanced NSCLC were included , 38 of whom were age >/= 70 years and 11 were age < 70 years but who had some contraindication to receiving cisplatin .

name: All patients were evaluable for response and toxicity .

name: Treatment was comprised of VNB , 25 mg / m ( 2 ), plus GEM , 1000 mg / m ( 2 ), both on Days 1 , 8 , and 15 every 28 days .

name: Patients received a minimum of three courses unless progressive disease was detected .

name: RESULTS : One hundred sixty - five courses were administered , with a median of 3 .

name: 6 courses per patient .

name: The overall response rate was 26 %( 95 % confidence interval , 15 - 41 %). Two patients attained a complete response ( 4 %) and 11 patients ( 22 %) achieved a partial response .

name: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status improved in 35 % of those patients with an initial value > 0 , whereas relief of at least 1 symptom without worsening of other symptoms was noted in 27 patients ( 55 %). label: TextChunk
name: The present studies demonstrate that growth and vitamin D treatment enhance the extent of artery calcification in rats given sufficient doses of Warfarin to inhibit gamma - carboxylation of matrix Gla protein , a calcification inhibitor known to be expressed by smooth muscle cells and macrophages in the artery wall .

label: Disease
name: calcification

label: TextChunk
name: The first series of experiments examined the influence of age and growth status on artery calcification in Warfarin - treated rats .

label: TextChunk
name: Treatment for 2 weeks with Warfarin caused massive focal calcification of the artery media in 20 - day - old rats and less extensive focal calcification in 42 - day - old rats .

label: Disease
name: calcification of the artery

label: TextChunk
name: In contrast , no artery calcification could be detected in 10 - month - old adult rats even after 4 weeks of Warfarin treatment .

label: TextChunk
name: To directly examine the importance of growth to Warfarin - induced artery calcification in animals of the same age , 20 - day - old rats were fed for 2 weeks either an ad libitum diet or a 6 - g / d restricted diet that maintains weight but prevents growth .

label: TextChunk
name: Concurrent treatment of both dietary groups with Warfarin produced massive focal calcification of the artery media in the ad libitum - fed rats but no detectable artery calcification in the restricted - diet , growth - inhibited group .

label: TextChunk
name: Although the explanation for the association between artery calcification and growth status cannot be determined from the present study , there was a relationship between higher serum phosphate and susceptibility to artery calcification , with 30 % higher levels of serum phosphate in young , ad libitum - fed rats compared with either of the groups that was resistant to Warfarin - induced artery calcification , ie , the 10 - month - old rats and the restricted - diet , growth - inhibited young rats .

label: Chemical
name: phosphate

label: TextChunk
name: This observation suggests that increased susceptibility to Warfarin - induced artery calcification could be related to higher serum phosphate levels .

label: TextChunk
name: The second set of experiments examined the possible synergy between vitamin D and Warfarin in artery calcification .

label: TextChunk
name: High doses of vitamin D are known to cause calcification of the artery media in as little as 3 to 4 days .

label: TextChunk
name: High doses of the vitamin K antagonist Warfarin are also known to cause calcification of the artery media , but at treatment times of 2 weeks or longer yet not at 1 week .

label: Chemical
name: vitamin K

label: TextChunk
name: In the current study , we investigated the synergy between these 2 treatments and found that concurrent Warfarin administration dramatically increased the extent of calcification in the media of vitamin D - treated rats at 3 and 4 days .

label: TextChunk
name: There was a close parallel between the effect of vitamin D dose on artery calcification and the effect of vitamin D dose on the elevation of serum calcium , which suggests that vitamin D may induce artery calcification through its effect on serum calcium .

label: Chemical
name: calcium

label: TextChunk
name: Because Warfarin treatment had no effect on the elevation in serum calcium produced by vitamin D , the synergy between Warfarin and vitamin D is probably best explained by the hypothesis that Warfarin inhibits the activity of matrix Gla protein as a calcification inhibitor .

label: TextChunk
name: High levels of matrix Gla protein are found at sites of artery calcification in rats treated with vitamin D plus Warfarin , and chemical analysis showed that the protein that accumulated was indeed not gamma-carboxylated .

label: Chemical
name: gamma-carboxylated

label: TextChunk
name: These observations indicate that although the gamma-carboxyglutamate residues of matrix Gla protein are apparently required for its function as a calcification inhibitor , they are not required for its accumulation at calcification

label: Chemical
name: gamma-carboxyglutamate

label: TextChunk
name: OBJECTIVE : To report 2 cases of ticlopidine - induced cholestatic hepatitis , investigate its mechanism , and compare the observed main characteristics with those of the published cases .

label: Chemical
name: ticlopidine

label: TextChunk
name: CASE SUMMARIES : Two patients developed prolonged cholestatic hepatitis after receiving ticlopidine following percutaneous coronary angioplasty , with complete remission during the follow - up period .

label: TextChunk
name: T - cell stimulation by therapeutic concentration of ticlopidine was demonstrated in vitro in the patients , but not in healthy controls .

label: TextChunk
name: DISCUSSION : Cholestatic hepatitis is a rare complication of the antiplatelet agent ticlopidine ; several cases have been reported but few in the English literature .

label: Disease
name: Cholestatic hepatitis

label: TextChunk
name: Our patients developed jaundice following treatment with ticlopidine and showed the clinical and laboratory characteristics of cholestatic hepatitis , which resolved after discontinuation of the drug .

label: Disease
name: jaundice

label: TextChunk
name: Hepatitis may develop weeks after discontinuation of the drug and may run a prolonged course , but complete remission was observed in all reported cases .

label: Disease
name: Hepatitis

label: TextChunk
name: An objective causality assessment revealed that the adverse drug event was probably related to the use of ticlopidine .

label: TextChunk
name: The mechanisms of this ticlopidine - induced cholestasis are unclear .

label: Disease
name: cholestasis

label: TextChunk
name: Immune mechanisms may be involved in the drug ' s hepatotoxicity , as suggested by the T - cell stimulation study reported here .

label: TextChunk
name: CONCLUSIONS : Cholestatic hepatitis is a rare adverse effect of ticlopidine that may be immune mediated .

label: TextChunk
name: Patients receiving the drug should be monitored with liver function tests along with complete blood cell counts .

label: TextChunk
name: This complication will be observed even less often in the future as ticlopidine is being replaced by the newer antiplatelet agent clopidogrel

name: Infusion regimens were designed that rapidly achieved and maintained target - free concentrations of these drugs in plasma and data on the relationship between free concentration and changes in MAPD were obtained for these compounds .

name: These data indicate that the free ED50 in plasma for terfenadine ( 1 .

label: TextChunk
name: 9 nM ), terodiline ( 76 nM ), cisapride ( 11 nM ) and E4031 ( 1 .

name: For compounds that have shown TDP in the clinic ( terfenadine , terodiline , cisapride ) there is little differentiation between the dog ED50 and the efficacious free plasma concentrations in man (< 10 - fold ) reflecting their limited safety margins .

label: TextChunk
name: The objective of this study was to determine the effects of maternal smoking on transient evoked otoacoustic emissions ( TEOAEs ) of healthy neonates .

label: TextChunk
name: METHODS : This study was undertaken as part of neonatal screening for hearing impairment and involved both ears of 200 newborns .

label: Disease
name: hearing impairment

label: TextChunk
name: Newborns whose mothers reported smoking during pregnancy ( n = 200 ears ) were compared to a control group of newborns ( n = 200 ears ), whose mothers were non - smokers .

label: TextChunk
name: Exposure to tobacco was characterized as low (< 5 cigarettes per day , n = 88 ears ), moderate ( 5 < or = cigarettes per day < 10 , n = 76 ) or high (> or = 10 cigarettes per day , n = 36 ). RESULTS : In exposed neonates , TEOAEs mean response ( across frequency ) and mean amplitude at 4000Hz was significantly lower than in non - exposed neonates .

label: TextChunk
name: Comparisons between exposed newborns ' subgroups revealed no significant differences .

label: TextChunk
name: However , by comparing each subgroup to control group , we found statistically significant decreases of TEOAEs amplitudes at 4000Hz for all three groups .

label: Disease
name: decreases of TEOAEs amplitudes

label: TextChunk
name: Mean TEOAEs responses of highly exposed newborns were also significantly lower in comparison to our control group .

label: TextChunk
name: CONCLUSION : In utero , exposure to tobacco smoking seems to have a small impact on outer hair cells .

label: TextChunk
name: These effects seem to be equally true for all exposed newborns , regardless of the degree of exposure .

label: TextChunk
name: Further studies are needed in order to establish a potential negative effect of maternal smoking

name: Treatments have included discontinuation or reduction of cyclosporine dose with or without concurrent plasma exchange , plasma infusion , anticoagulation , and intravenous immunoglobulin G infusion .

name: However , for recipients of organ transplantation , removing the inciting agent is not without the attendant risk of precipitating acute rejection and graft loss .

label: TextChunk
name: The last decade has seen the emergence of tacrolimus as a potent immunosuppressive agent with mechanisms of action virtually identical to those of cyclosporine .

label: TextChunk
name: As a result , switching to tacrolimus has been reported to be a viable therapeutic option in the setting of cyclosporine - induced TMA .

label: TextChunk
name: With the more widespread application of tacrolimus in organ transplantation , tacrolimus - associated TMA has also been recognized .

label: TextChunk
name: However , literature regarding the incidence of the recurrence of TMA in patients exposed sequentially to cyclosporine and tacrolimus is limited .

label: TextChunk
name: We report a case of a living donor renal transplant recipient who developed cyclosporine - induced TMA that responded to the withdrawal of cyclosporine in conjunction with plasmapheresis and fresh frozen plasma replacement therapy .

label: TextChunk
name: Introduction of tacrolimus as an alternative immunosuppressive agent resulted in the recurrence of TMA and the subsequent loss of the renal allograft .

label: TextChunk
name: Patients who are switched from cyclosporine to tacrolimus or vice versa should be closely monitored for the signs and symptoms of recurrent TMA

name: Adverse effects of topical papaverine on auditory nerve function .

label: TextChunk
name: BACKGROUND : Papaverine hydrochloride is a direct - acting vasodilator used to manage vasospasm during various neurosurgical operations .

label: Chemical
name: Papaverine hydrochloride

label: TextChunk
name: Transient cranial nerve dysfunction has been described in a few cases with topical papaverine .

label: Disease
name: cranial nerve dysfunction

label: TextChunk
name: This study supports previous reports and provides neurophysiological evidence of an adverse effect on the auditory nerve .

label: TextChunk
name: METHODS : We conducted a retrospective review of 70 consecutive microvascular decompression operations and studied those patients who received topical papaverine for vasospasm .

label: TextChunk
name: Topical papaverine was used as a direct therapeutic action to manage vasospasm in a total of 11 patients .

label: TextChunk
name: The timing of papaverine application and ongoing operative events was reviewed relative to changes in neurophysiological recordings .

label: TextChunk
name: Brainstem auditory evoked potentials ( BAEPs ) were routinely used to monitor cochlear nerve function during these operations .

label: TextChunk
name: FINDINGS : A temporal relationship was found between topical papaverine and BAEP changes leading to complete waveform loss .

label: TextChunk
name: The average temporal delay between papaverine and the onset of an adverse BAEP change was 5 min .

label: TextChunk
name: In 10 of 11 patients , BAEP waves II / III - V completely disappeared within 2 to 25 min after papaverine .

label: TextChunk
name: Eight of these 10 patients had complete loss of BAEP waveforms within 10 min .

label: TextChunk
name: One patient showed no recovery of later waves and a delayed profound sensorineural hearing loss .

label: TextChunk
name: The average recovery time of BAEP waveforms to pre - papaverine baseline values was 39 min .

label: TextChunk
name: CONCLUSIONS : Topical papaverine for the treatment of vasospasm was associated with the onset of a transient disturbance in neurophysiological function of the ascending auditory brainstem pathway .

label: TextChunk
name: The complete disappearance of BAEP waveforms with a consistent temporal delay suggests a possible adverse effect on the proximal eighth nerve .

label: TextChunk
name: BACKGROUND : Sirolimus is the latest immunosuppressive agent used to prevent rejection , and may have less nephrotoxicity than calcineurin inhibitor ( CNI )- based regimens .

label: TextChunk
name: To date there has been little documentation of clinically significant proteinuria linked with the use of sirolimus .

label: TextChunk
name: We have encountered several patients who developed substantial proteinuria associated with sirolimus use .

label: TextChunk
name: In each patient , the close temporal association between the commencement of sirolimus therapy and proteinuria implicated sirolimus as the most likely etiology of the proteinuria .

label: TextChunk
name: METHODS : We analyzed the clinical and laboratory information available for all 119 patients transplanted at the Washington Hospital Center between 1999 - 2003 for whom sirolimus was a component of their immunosuppressant regimen .

label: TextChunk
name: In these patients , the magnitude of proteinuria was assessed on morning urine samples by turbidometric measurement or random urine protein : creatinine ratios , an estimate of grams of proteinuria / day .

label: TextChunk
name: Laboratory results were compared between prior , during and following sirolimus use .

label: TextChunk
name: RESULTS : Twenty - eight patients ( 24 %) developed increased proteinuria from baseline during their post - transplantation course .

label: TextChunk
name: In 21 patients an alternative cause of proteinuria was either obvious or insufficient data was available to be conclusive .

label: TextChunk
name: In 7 of the 28 patients there was a striking temporal association between the initiation of sirolimus and the development of nephrotic - range proteinuria .

label: TextChunk
name: Proteinuria correlated most strongly with sirolimus therapy when compared to other demographic and clinical variables .

label: TextChunk
name: In most patients , discontinuation of sirolimus resulted in a decrease , but not resolution , of proteinuria .

label: TextChunk
name: CONCLUSIONS : Sirolimus induces or aggravates pre - existing proteinuria in an unpredictable subset of renal allograft recipients .

name: Proteinuria may improve , but does not resolve , when sirolimus

name: BACKGROUND : Electrocardiography has a very low sensitivity in detecting dobutamine - induced myocardial ischemia .

name: Tc99m-Sestamibi

name: METHODS : The study group comprised 40 patients undergoing Sestamibi - SPECT / dobutamine stress test .

label: Chemical
name: Sestamibi

name: Simultaneous measurements of ECG and brachial artery dP / dtejc were performed at each dobutamine level .

label: TextChunk
name: In 19 of the 40 patients perfusion defects compatible with ischemia were detected on SPECT .

label: TextChunk
name: The increase in dP / dtejc during infusion of dobutamine in this group was severely impaired as compared to the non - ischemic group .

label: TextChunk
name: dP / dtejc outcome was combined with the ECG results , giving an ECG - enhanced value , and compared to ECG alone .

label: TextChunk
name: RESULTS : The sensitivity improved dramatically from 16 % to 79 %, positive predictive value increased from 60 % to 68 % and negative predictive value from 54 % to 78 %, and specificity decreased from 90 % to 67 %. CONCLUSIONS : If ECG alone is used for specificity , the combination with dP / dtejc improved the sensitivity of the test and could be a cost - savings alternative to cardiac imaging or perfusion studies to detect myocardial ischemia

name: OBJECTIVE : The goal of this study was to compare the efficacy and side effects of two doses of haloperidol and placebo in the treatment of psychosis and disruptive behaviors in patients with Alzheimer's disease .

label: TextChunk

name: psychomotor agitation

label: TextChunk
name: Response rates according to three sets of criteria were greater with the standard dose ( 55 %- 60 %) than the low dose ( 25 %- 35 %) and placebo ( 25 %- 30 %). The advantage of standard dose over low dose was replicated in phase B .

name: In phase A , extrapyramidal signs tended to be greater with the standard dose than in the other two conditions , primarily because of a subgroup ( 20 %) who developed moderate to severe signs .

label: Disease
name: extrapyramidal signs

label: TextChunk
name: Low - dose haloperidol did not differ from placebo on any measure of efficacy or side effects .

label: TextChunk
name: CONCLUSIONS : The results indicated a favorable therapeutic profile for haloperidol in doses of 2 - 3 mg / day , although a subgroup developed moderate to severe extrapyramidal signs .

label: TextChunk
name: A starting dose of 1 mg / day with gradual , upward dose titration is recommended .

label: TextChunk
name: The narrow therapeutic window observed with haloperidol may also apply to other neuroleptics used in Alzheimer's disease patients with psychosis and disruptive behaviors

name: A few minutes after administration of the drugs , they presented urticaria ( patients 1 and 2 ) and conjunctivitis ( patient 1 ). name: conjunctivitis

label: TextChunk
name: Clinical examinations and skin , oral and parenteral challenges with different corticosteroids and ELISA tests were performed .

name: In the two patients , skin and ELISA tests with paramethasone were negative , as was the prick test with each of its excipients .

label: TextChunk
name: A single - blind parenteral challenge with Triniol was positive in both patients after the administration of 1 ml of the drug , and negative with its excipients .

label: TextChunk
name: We also carried out oral and parenteral challenges with other corticosteroids and found intolerance to some of them .

label: TextChunk
name: These results suggest that paramethasone caused pseudoallergic reactions in our patients .

label: TextChunk
name: Corticosteroids different from paramethasone also produced hypersensitivity reactions in these patients ; however , a few of them were tolerated .

label: TextChunk
name: The basic mechanisms of those reactions are not yet fully understood .

label: TextChunk
name: To our knowledge , this is the first report of a pseudo - allergy caused by paramethasone

label: TextChunk
name: MATERIALS AND METHODS : From November 2005 to September 2007 , 8 patients ( 5 men and 3 women ) were diagnosed as having metronidazole - induced encephalopathy ( age range ; 43 - 78 years ). They had been taking metronidazole ( total dosage , 45 - 120 g ; duration , 30 days to 2 months ) to treat the infection in various organs .

name: Initial brain magnetic resonance imaging ( MRI ) were obtained after the hospitalization , including DWI ( 8 / 8 ), apparent diffusion coefficient ( ADC ) map ( 4 / 8 ), FLAIR ( 7 / 8 ), and T2 - weighted image ( 8 / 8 ). Follow - up MRIs were performed on 5 patients from third to 14th days after discontinuation of metronidazole administration .

label: TextChunk
name: Findings of initial and follow - up MRIs were retrospectively evaluated by 2 neuroradiologists by consensus , to analyze the presence of abnormal signal intensities , their locations , and signal changes on follow - up images .

label: TextChunk
name: RESULTS : Initial MRIs showed abnormal high signal intensities on DWI and FLAIR ( or T2 - weighted image ) at the dentate nucleus ( 8 / 8 ), inferior colliculus ( 6 / 8 ), corpus callosum ( 2 / 8 ), pons ( 2 / 8 ), medulla ( 1 / 8 ), and bilateral cerebral white matter ( 1 / 8 ). High - signal intensity lesions on DWI tended to show low signal intensity on ADC map ( 3 / 4 ), but in one patient , high signal intensity was shown at bilateral dentate nuclei on not only DWI but also ADC map .

label: TextChunk
name: All the lesions in dentate , inferior colliculus , pons , and medullas had been resolved completely on follow - up MRIs in 5 patients , but in 1 patient of them , corpus callosal lesion persisted .

label: Disease
name: callosal lesion

label: TextChunk
name: CONCLUSIONS : Reversible inferior colliculus lesions could be considered as the characteristic for metronidazole - induced encephalopathy

name: The haemodynamic effects of propofol in combination with ephedrine in elderly patients ( ASA groups 3 and 4 ). The marked vasodilator and negative inotropic effects of propofol are disadvantages in frail elderly patients .

name: We investigated the safety and efficacy of adding different doses of ephedrine to propofol in order to obtund the hypotensive response .

label: TextChunk
name: The haemodynamic effects of adding 15 , 20 or 25 mg of ephedrine to 200 mg of propofol were compared to control in 40 ASA 3 / 4 patients over 60 years presenting for genito - urinary surgery .

label: TextChunk
name: The addition of ephedrine to propofol appears to be an effective method of obtunding the hypotensive response to propofol at all doses used in this study .

label: TextChunk
name: However , marked tachycardia associated with the use of ephedrine in combination with propofol occurred in the majority of patients , occasionally reaching high levels in individual patients .

label: TextChunk
name: Due to the risk of this tachycardia inducing myocardial ischemia , we would not recommend the use in elderly patients of any of the ephedrine / propofol

label: TextChunk
name: We initiated a phase I / II trial to determine the response and toxicity of escalating paclitaxel doses combined with fixed - dose cisplatin with granulocyte colony - stimulating factor support in patients with untreated locally advanced inoperable head and neck carcinoma .

label: TextChunk
name: To date , 23 men with a median age of 50 years and good performance status have entered the trial .

label: TextChunk
name: Primary tumor sites were oropharynx , 10 patients ; hypopharynx , four ; larynx , two ; oral cavity , three ; unknown primary , two ; and nasal cavity and parotid gland , one each .

label: TextChunk
name: Of 20 patients evaluable for toxicity , four had stage III and 16 had stage IV disease .

label: TextChunk
name: Treatment , given every 21 days for a maximum of three cycles , consisted of paclitaxel by 3 - hour infusion followed the next day by a fixed dose of cisplatin ( 75 mg / m2 ). The dose levels incorporate escalating paclitaxel doses , and intrapatient escalations within a given dose level are permitted if toxicity permits .

label: TextChunk
name: At the time of this writing , dose level 4 ( 260 , 270 , and 280 mg / m2 ) is being evaluated ; three patients from this level are evaluable .

label: TextChunk
name: With paclitaxel doses of 200 mg / m2 and higher , granulocyte colony - stimulating factor 5 micrograms / kg / d is given ( days 4 through 12 ). Of 18 patients evaluable for response , seven ( 39 %) achieved a complete response and six ( 33 %) achieved a partial response .

label: TextChunk
name: Three patients had no change and disease progressed in two .

label: TextChunk
name: The overall response rate is 72 %. Eleven responding patients had subsequent surgery / radiotherapy or radical radiotherapy .

label: TextChunk
name: Two pathologic complete responses were observed in patients who had achieved clinical complete responses .

label: TextChunk
name: Alopecia , paresthesias , and arthralgias / myalgias have occurred frequently , but with one exception ( a grade 3 myalgia ) they have been grade 1 or 2 .

label: TextChunk
name: We used an in vitro model of chemotherapy induced peripheral neuropathy that closely mimic the in vivo condition by exposing primary cultures of dorsal root ganglion ( DRG ) sensory neurons to paclitaxel and cisplatin , two widely used and highly effective chemotherapeutic drugs .

name: In conclusion mitochondrial toxicity is an early common event both in paclitaxel and cisplatin induced neurotoxicity .

label: TextChunk
name: All three cytopenias were completely reversible following cessation of treatment ; the time required for recovery of the erythron ( approximately 1 month ) was considerably longer than that of the granulocytes and platelets ( hours to a few days ). Upon rechallenge with either cephalosporin , the hematologic syndrome was reproduced in most dogs tested ; cefonicid ( but not cefazedone )- treated dogs showed a substantially reduced induction period ( 15 +/- 5 days ) compared to that of the first exposure to the drug ( 61 +/- 24 days ). This observation , along with the rapid rate of decline in red cell mass parameters of affected dogs , suggests that a hemolytic component complicated the red cell production problem and that multiple toxicologic mechanisms contributed to the cytopenia .

label: TextChunk

label: TextChunk
name: Both agents show substantial clinical efficacy , with reductions in total cholesterol of over 30 % and in LDL - cholesterol of 40 % in clinical studies .

label: TextChunk

label: TextChunk
name: Clinical tolerability of both agents has been good , with fewer than 3 % of patients withdrawn from treatment because of clinical adverse experiences .

label: TextChunk
name: Ophthalmological examinations in over 1100 patients treated with one or the other agent have revealed no evidence of significant short term ( up to 2 years ) cataractogenic potential .

label: TextChunk
name: One to 2 % of patients have elevations of serum transaminases to greater than 3 times the upper limit of normal .

label: TextChunk
name: These episodes are asymptomatic and reversible when therapy is discontinued .

label: TextChunk
name: Minor elevations of creatine kinase levels are reported in about 5 % of patients .

label: TextChunk

name: A Phase II trial of cisplatin plus WR-2721 ( amifostine ) for metastatic breast carcinoma : an Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group Study ( E8188 ). BACKGROUND : Cisplatin has minimal antitumor activity when used as second - or third - line treatment of metastatic breast carcinoma .

label: TextChunk
name: Older reports suggest an objective response rate of 8 % when 60 - 120 mg / m2 of cisplatin is administered every 3 - 4 weeks .

name: Although a dose - response effect has been observed with cisplatin , the dose - limiting toxicities associated with cisplatin ( e .

label: TextChunk
name: g ., nephrotoxicity , ototoxicity , and neurotoxicity ) have limited its use as a treatment for breast carcinoma .

label: TextChunk
name: WR-2721 or amifostine initially was developed to protect military personnel in the event of nuclear war .

label: TextChunk
name: Amifostine subsequently was shown to protect normal tissues from the toxic effects of alkylating agents and cisplatin without decreasing the antitumor effect of the chemotherapy .

label: Chemical
name: Amifostine

label: TextChunk
name: Early trials of cisplatin and amifostine also suggested that the incidence and severity of cisplatin - induced nephrotoxicity , ototoxicity , and neuropathy were reduced .

label: TextChunk
name: METHODS : A Phase II study of the combination of cisplatin plus amifostine was conducted in patients with progressive metastatic breast carcinoma who had received one , but not more than one , chemotherapy regimen for metastatic disease .

label: TextChunk
name: Patients received amifostine , 910 mg / m2 intravenously over 15 minutes .

label: TextChunk
name: After completion of the amifostine infusion , cisplatin 120 mg / m2 was administered over 30 minutes .

label: TextChunk
name: Intravenous hydration and mannitol was administered before and after cisplatin .

label: Chemical
name: mannitol

label: TextChunk
name: Treatment was administered every 3 weeks until disease progression .

label: TextChunk
name: RESULTS : Forty - four patients were enrolled in the study of which 7 ( 16 %) were ineligible .

label: TextChunk
name: A median of 2 cycles of therapy was administered to the 37 eligible patients .

label: TextChunk
name: Six partial responses were observed for an overall response rate of 16 %. Most patients ( 57 %) stopped treatment because of disease progression .

label: TextChunk
name: Neurologic toxicity was reported in 52 % of patients .

label: Disease
name: Neurologic toxicity

label: TextChunk
name: Seven different life - threatening toxicities were observed in patients while receiving treatment .

label: TextChunk
name: CONCLUSIONS : The combination of cisplatin and amifostine in this study resulted in an overall response rate of 16 %. Neither a tumor - protective effect nor reduced toxicity to normal tissues was observed with the addition of amifostine to cisplatin

name: We report the case of a 30 - year - old Caucasian man who came to the emergency department in atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response .

name: His medical history was unremarkable , except for minor fractures of the fingers and foot .

name: Hepatic reactions associated with ketoconazole in the United Kingdom .

label: TextChunk
name: Ketoconazole was introduced in the United Kingdom in 1981 .

label: TextChunk
name: By November 1984 the Committee on Safety of Medicines had received 82 reports of possible hepatotoxicity associated with the drug , including five deaths .

label: TextChunk
name: An analysis of the 75 cases that had been adequately followed up suggested that 16 , including three deaths , were probably related to treatment with the drug .

label: TextChunk
name: Of the remainder , 48 were possibly related to treatment , five were unlikely to be so , and six were unclassifiable .

label: TextChunk
name: The mean age of patients in the 16 probable cases was 57 .

label: TextChunk
name: 9 , with hepatotoxicity being more common in women .

label: TextChunk
name: The average duration of treatment before the onset of jaundice was 61 days .

label: TextChunk
name: of these well validated cases occurred within the first 10 days after treatment .

label: TextChunk
name: The results of serum liver function tests suggested hepatocellular injury in 10 ( 63 %); the rest showed a mixed pattern .

label: Disease
name: hepatocellular injury

label: TextChunk
name: In contrast , the results of histological examination of the liver often showed evidence of cholestasis .

label: TextChunk
name: The characteristics of the 48 patients in the possible cases were similar .

label: TextChunk
name: Allergic manifestations such as rash and eosinophilia were rare .

label: TextChunk
name: Hepatitis was usually reversible when treatment was stopped , with the results of liver function tests returning to normal after an average of 3 .

label: TextChunk
name: 1 months .

label: TextChunk
name: In two of the three deaths probably associated with ketoconazole treatment the drug had been continued after the onset of jaundice and other symptoms of hepatitis .

label: TextChunk
name: Clinical and biochemical monitoring at regular intervals for evidence of hepatitis is advised during long term treatment with ketoconazole to prevent possible serious hepatic injury

name: Levodopa is the most effective drug for the treatment of Parkinson's disease .

name: However , the long - term use of this dopamine precursor is complicated by highly disabling fluctuations and dyskinesias .

label: TextChunk
name: Although preclinical and clinical findings suggest pulsatile stimulation of striatal postsynaptic receptors as a key mechanism underlying levodopa - induced dyskinesias , their pathogenesis is still unclear .

label: TextChunk
name: In recent years , evidence from animal models of Parkinson's disease has provided important information to understand the effect of specific receptor and post - receptor molecular mechanisms underlying the development of dyskinetic movements .

name: Prevention of seizures and reorganization of hippocampal functions by transplantation of bone marrow cells in the acute phase of experimental epilepsy .

label: TextChunk
name: In this study , we investigated the therapeutic potential of bone marrow mononuclear cells ( BMCs ) in a model of epilepsy induced by pilocarpine in rats .

label: TextChunk
name: BMCs obtained from green fluorescent protein ( GFP ) transgenic mice or rats were transplanted intravenously after induction of status epilepticus ( SE ). Spontaneous recurrent seizures ( SRS ) were monitored using Racine ' s seizure severity scale .

label: Disease
name: Spontaneous recurrent seizures

label: Disease
name: SRS

label: TextChunk
name: All of the rats in the saline - treated epileptic control group developed SRS , whereas none of the BMC - treated epileptic animals had seizures in the short term ( 15 days after transplantation ), regardless of the BMC source .

label: TextChunk
name: Over the long - term chronic phase ( 120 days after transplantation ), only 25 % of BMC - treated epileptic animals had seizures , but with a lower frequency and duration compared to the epileptic control group .

label: TextChunk
name: The density of hippocampal neurons in the brains of animals treated with BMCs was markedly preserved .

label: TextChunk
name: At hippocampal Schaeffer collateral - CA1 synapses , long - term potentiation was preserved in BMC - transplanted rats compared to epileptic controls .

label: TextChunk
name: The donor - derived GFP (+) cells were rarely found in the brains of transplanted epileptic rats .

label: TextChunk
name: In conclusion , treatment with BMCs can prevent the development of chronic seizures , reduce neuronal loss

label: TextChunk
name: The present study was designed to evaluate the cardioprotective potential of salvianolic acid A on isoproterenol - induced myocardial infarction in rats .

name: Hemodynamic parameters and lead II electrocardiograph were monitored and recorded continuously .

label: TextChunk
name: Cardiac marker enzymes and antioxidative parameters in serum and heart tissues were measured .

label: TextChunk
name: Assay for mitochondrial respiratory function and histopathological examination of heart tissues were performed .

label: TextChunk
name: Isoproterenol - treated rats showed significant increases in the levels of lactate dehydrogenase , aspartate transaminase , creatine kinase and malondialdehyde and significant decreases in the activities of superoxide dismutase , catalase and glutathione peroxidase in serum and heart .

name: These rats also showed declines in left ventricular systolic pressure , maximum and minimum rate of developed left ventricular pressure , and elevation of left ventricular end - diastolic pressure and ST - segment .

name: respiratory dysfunction

label: TextChunk
name: Administration of salvianolic acid A for a period of 8 days significantly attenuated isoproterenol - induced cardiac dysfunction and myocardial injury and improved mitochondrial respiratory function .

label: TextChunk
name: The protective role of salvianolic acid A against isoproterenol - induced myocardial damage was further confirmed by histopathological examination .

label: TextChunk
name: The results of our study suggest that salvianolic acid A possessing antioxidant activity has a significant protective effect against isoproterenol - induced myocardial infarction

name: amino acid

label: TextChunk
name: The present study aimed to investigate the anticonvulsant activity as well as the effects on the level of hippocampal amino acid neurotransmitters ( glutamate , aspartate , glycine and GABA ) of N-(2-propylpentanoyl)urea ( VPU ) in comparison to its parent compound , valproic acid ( VPA ). VPU was more potent than VPA , exhibiting the median effective dose ( ED ( 50 )) of 49 mg / kg in protecting rats against pilocarpine - induced seizure whereas the corresponding value for VPA was 322 mg / kg .

label: Chemical
name: VPU

label: TextChunk
name: In vivo microdialysis demonstrated that an intraperitoneal administration of pilocarpine induced a pronounced increment of hippocampal glutamate and aspartate whereas no significant change was observed on the level of glycine and GABA .

label: TextChunk
name: Pretreatment with either VPU ( 50 and 100 mg / kg ) or VPA ( 300 and 600 mg / kg ) completely abolished pilocarpine - evoked increases in extracellular glutamate and aspartate .

label: TextChunk
name: In addition , a statistically significant reduction was also observed on the level of GABA and glycine but less than a drastic reduction of glutamate and aspartate level .

label: TextChunk
name: Based on the finding that VPU and VPA could protect the animals against pilocarpine - induced seizure it is suggested that the reduction of inhibitory amino acid neurotransmitters was comparatively minor and offset by a pronounced reduction of glutamate and aspartate .

label: TextChunk
name: Therefore , like VPA , the finding that VPU could drastically reduce pilocarpine - induced increases in glutamate and aspartate should account , at least partly , for its anticonvulsant activity observed in pilocarpine - induced seizure

label: TextChunk
name: INTRODUCTION : Antibiotic - associated hepatotoxicity is rare .

label: TextChunk
name: The patient had suffered a previous episode of " acute hepatitis of unknown origin ," that occurred after telithromycin usage .

label: TextChunk
name: Both incidents occurred within a year .

label: TextChunk
name: DISCUSSION : Telithromycin is the first of the ketolide antibacterials to receive US Food and Drug Administration approval for clinical use .

label: Chemical
name: Telithromycin

label: TextChunk
name: It has been associated with infrequent and usually reversible severe hepatic dysfunction .

label: TextChunk
name: Based on a score of 8 on the Naranjo adverse drug reaction probability scale , telithromycin was the probable cause of acute hepatitis in this patient , and pathological findings suggested drug - induced toxic hepatitis .

label: Disease

label: TextChunk

label: TextChunk
name: CONCLUSION : Here we report a case of acute hepatitis probably associated with the administration of telithromycin

name: venlafaxine overdose

label: TextChunk
name: OBJECTIVE : To report a case of venlafaxine overdose .

label: TextChunk
name: CASE SUMMARY : A 40 - year - old woman with major depression took an overdose of venlafaxine in an apparent suicide attempt .

label: TextChunk
name: After the ingestion of 26 venlafaxine 50 - mg tablets , the patient experienced a witnessed generalized seizure .

label: TextChunk
name: She was admitted to the medical intensive care unit , venlafaxine was discontinued , and no further sequelae were seen .

label: TextChunk
name: DISCUSSION : To our knowledge , this is the first reported case of venlafaxine overdose that resulted in a generalized seizure .

label: TextChunk
name: Based on nonoverdose pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics of venlafaxine and the potential risks of available interventions , no emergent therapy was instituted .

label: TextChunk
name: CONCLUSIONS : The venlafaxine overdose in our patient resulted in a single episode of generalized seizure

label: TextChunk
name: BE - Induced seizures occurred more frequently and had significantly longer latencies than those induced by equimolar amounts of cocaine .

name: Whereas cocaine - induced seizures were best characterized as brief , generalized , and tonic and resulted in death , those induced by BE were prolonged , often multiple and mixed in type , and rarely resulted in death .

label: TextChunk
name: Blood pressure ( BP ) is more salt sensitive in men than in premenopausal women .

name: In Dahl salt - sensitive rats ( DS ), high - salt ( HS ) diet increases BP more in males than females .

label: TextChunk
name: In contrast to the systemic renin - angiotensin system , which is suppressed in response to HS in male DS , intrarenal angiotensinogen expression is increased , and intrarenal levels of ANG II are not suppressed .

label: TextChunk
name: In this study , the hypothesis was tested that there is a sexual dimorphism in HS - induced upregulation of intrarenal angiotensinogen mediated by testosterone that also causes increases in BP and renal injury .

name: On a low - salt ( LS ) diet , male DS had higher levels of intrarenal angiotensinogen mRNA than females .

label: TextChunk
name: HS diet for 4 wk increased renal cortical angiotensinogen mRNA and protein only in male DS , which was prevented by castration .

label: TextChunk
name: Ovariectomy of female DS had no effect on intrarenal angiotensinogen expression on either diet .

label: TextChunk
name: Radiotelemetric BP was similar between males and castrated rats on LS diet .

label: TextChunk
name: HS diet for 4 wk caused a progressive increase in BP , protein and albumin excretion , and glomerular sclerosis in male DS rats , which were attenuated by castration .

label: TextChunk
name: Testosterone replacement in castrated DS rats increased BP , renal injury , and upregulation of renal angiotensinogen associated with HS diet .

name: Testosterone contributes to the development of hypertension and renal injury in male DS rats on HS diet possibly through upregulation of the intrarenal renin - angiotensin

name: The goal of these studies was to use an animal model to examine the effects of prenatal protein deprivation on behaviors and receptor binding with relevance to schizophrenia .

label: TextChunk
name: We report that prenatally protein deprived ( PD ) female rats showed an increased stereotypic response to apomorphine and an increased locomotor response to amphetamine in adulthood .

name: No changes in haloperidol - induced catalepsy or MK-801 - induced locomotion were seen following PD .

name: mToR inhibitors - induced proteinuria : mechanisms , significance , and management .

label: TextChunk
name: Massive urinary protein excretion has been observed after conversion from calcineurin inhibitors to mammalian target of rapamycin ( mToR ) inhibitors , especially sirolimus , in renal transplant recipients with chronic allograft nephropathy .

label: TextChunk
name: Because proteinuria is a major predictive factor of poor transplantation outcome , many studies focused on this adverse event during the past years .

label: TextChunk
name: Whether proteinuria was due to sirolimus or only a consequence of calcineurin inhibitors withdrawal remained unsolved until high range proteinuria has been observed during sirolimus therapy in islet transplantation and in patients who received sirolimus de novo .

label: TextChunk
name: Podocyte injury and focal segmental glomerulosclerosis have been related to mToR inhibition in some patients , but the pathways underlying these lesions remain hypothetic .

label: TextChunk
name: We discuss herein the possible mechanisms and the significance of mToR blockade - induced proteinuria

name: Upregulation of brain expression of P - glycoprotein in MRP2 - deficient TR (-) rats resembles seizure - induced up - regulation of this drug efflux transporter in normal rats .

label: TextChunk
name: PURPOSE : The multidrug resistance protein 2 ( MRP2 ) is a drug efflux transporter that is expressed predominantly at the apical domain of hepatocytes but seems also to be expressed at the apical membrane of brain capillary endothelial cells that form the blood - brain barrier ( BBB ). MRP2 is absent in the transport - deficient ( TR (-)) Wistar rat mutant , so that this rat strain was very helpful in defining substrates of MRP2 by comparing tissue concentrations or functional activities of compounds in MRP2 - deficient rats with those in transport - competent Wistar rats .

label: TextChunk
name: By using this strategy to study the involvement of MRP2 in brain access of antiepileptic drugs ( AEDs ), we recently reported that phenytoin is a substrate for MRP2 in the BBB .

label: TextChunk
name: However , one drawback of such studies in genetically deficient rats is the fact that compensatory changes with upregulation of other transporters can occur .

label: TextChunk
name: This prompted us to study the brain expression of P - glycoprotein ( Pgp ), a major drug efflux transporter in many tissues , including the BBB , in TR (-) rats compared with nonmutant ( wild - type ) Wistar rats .

label: TextChunk
name: METHODS : The expression of MRP2 and Pgp in brain and liver sections of TR (-) rats and normal Wistar rats was determined with immunohistochemistry , by using a novel , highly selective monoclonal MRP2 antibody and the monoclonal Pgp antibody C219 , respectively .

label: TextChunk
name: RESULTS : Immunofluorescence staining with the MRP2 antibody was found to label a high number of microvessels throughout the brain in normal Wistar rats , whereas such labeling was absent in TR (-) rats .

label: TextChunk
name: TR (-) rats exhibited a significant up - regulation of Pgp in brain capillary endothelial cells compared with wild - type controls .

label: TextChunk
name: No such obvious upregulation of Pgp was observed in liver sections .

label: TextChunk
name: A comparable overexpression of Pgp in the BBB was obtained after pilocarpine - induced seizures in wild - type Wistar rats .

label: TextChunk
name: Experiments with systemic administration of the Pgp substrate phenobarbital and the selective Pgp inhibitor tariquidar in TR (-) rats substantiated that Pgp is functional and compensates for the lack of MRP2 in the BBB .

label: Chemical
name: phenobarbital

label: Chemical
name: tariquidar

label: TextChunk
name: CONCLUSIONS : The data on TR (-) rats indicate that Pgp plays an important role in the compensation of MRP2 deficiency in the BBB .

label: TextChunk
name: Because such a compensatory mechanism most likely occurs to reduce injury to the brain from cytotoxic compounds , the present data substantiate the concept that MRP2 performs a protective role in the BBB .

label: Disease
name: injury to the brain

label: TextChunk
name: Furthermore , our data suggest that TR (-) rats are an interesting tool to study consequences of overexpression of Pgp in the BBB on access of drugs in the brain , without the need of inducing seizures

label: TextChunk

label: TextChunk
name: CSS mapping suggests seizure susceptibility loci on mouse Chromosomes 10 and 18 .

name: For three patients , the decision was made not to proceed with angiography after positive preangiographic testing .

name: The dosage of indocyanine green used for angiography varied from 25 to 75 mg , depending upon the institution .

label: TextChunk
name: Different mechanisms have been suggested for cocaine toxicity including an increase in oxidative stress but the association between oxidative status in the brain and cocaine induced - behaviour is poorly understood .

label: TextChunk
name: Nuclear factor kappa B ( NFkappaB ) is a sensor of oxidative stress and participates in memory formation that could be involved in drug toxicity and addiction mechanisms .

label: TextChunk
name: Therefore NFkappaB activity , oxidative stress , neuronal nitric oxide synthase ( nNOS ) activity , spatial learning and memory as well as the effect of topiramate , a previously proposed therapy for cocaine addiction , were evaluated in an experimental model of cocaine administration in rats .

label: Disease
name: cocaine addiction

label: TextChunk
name: NFkappaB activity was decreased in the frontal cortex of cocaine treated rats , as well as GSH concentration and glutathione peroxidase activity in the hippocampus , whereas nNOS activity in the hippocampus was increased .

label: TextChunk
name: Memory retrieval of experiences acquired prior to cocaine administration was impaired and negatively correlated with NFkappaB activity in the frontal cortex .

label: TextChunk
name: In contrast , learning of new tasks was enhanced and correlated with the increase of nNOS activity and the decrease of glutathione peroxidase .

label: TextChunk
name: These results provide evidence for a possible mechanistic role of oxidative and nitrosative stress and NFkappaB in the alterations induced by cocaine .

name: The antiepileptic drugs , phenobarbitone and carbamazepine are well known to cause cognitive impairment on chronic use .

name: Curcumin has shown antioxidant , anti - inflammatory and neuro - protective properties .

name: Therefore , the present study was carried out to investigate the effect of chronic curcumin administration on phenobarbitone - and carbamazepine - induced cognitive impairment and oxidative stress in rats .

name: At the end of study period , serum phenobarbitone and carbamazepine , whole brain malondialdehyde and reduced glutathione levels were estimated .

name: Using macaque monkeys with different types of MPTP - induced parkinsonism , the current study evaluated the degree to which rate of symptom progression , symptom severity , and response to and duration of levodopa therapy may be involved in the development of LIDs .

name: Monkeys with acute ( short - term ) MPTP exposure , rapid symptom onset and short symptom duration prior to initiation of levodopa therapy developed dyskinesia between 11 and 24 days of daily levodopa administration .

label: TextChunk
name: In contrast , monkeys with long - term MPTP exposure , slow symptom progression and / or long symptom duration prior to initiation of levodopa therapy were more resistant to developing LIDs ( e .

label: TextChunk
name: g ., dyskinesia developed no sooner than 146 days of chronic levodopa administration ). All animals were similarly symptomatic at the start of levodopa treatment and had similar therapeutic responses to the drug .

label: TextChunk
name: These data suggest distinct differences in the propensity to develop LIDs in monkeys with different rates of symptom progression or symptom durations prior to levodopa and demonstrate the value of these models for further studying the pathophysiology of LIDs

label: TextChunk
name: PURPOSE : We reviewed the results of serum testosterone and prolactin determination in 1 , 022 patients referred because of erectile dysfunction and compared the data with history , results of physical examination , other etiological investigations and effects of endocrine therapy to refine the rules of cost - effective endocrine screening and to pinpoint actual responsibility for hormonal abnormalities .

label: TextChunk

Determining testosterone only in cases of low sexual desire or abnormal physical examination would have missed 40 % of the cases with low testosterone , including 37 % of those subsequently improved by androgen therapy .

label: TextChunk
name: CONCLUSIONS : Low prevalences and effects of low testosterone and high prolactin in erectile dysfunction cannot justify their routine determination .

label: TextChunk

label: TextChunk

name: The aim of this study was to investigate the effect of Rg1 on learning impairment by chronic morphine administration and the mechanism responsible for this effect .

label: TextChunk
name: Male rats were subcutaneously injected with morphine ( 10 mg / kg ) twice a day at 12 hour intervals for 10 days , and Rg1 ( 30 mg / kg ) was intraperitoneally injected 2 hours after the second injection of morphine once a day for 10 days .

label: TextChunk
name: Spatial learning capacity was assessed in the Morris water maze .

label: TextChunk
name: The results showed that rats treated with Morphine / Rg1 decreased escape latency and increased the time spent in platform quadrant and entering frequency .

label: Chemical
name: Morphine

label: TextChunk
name: By implantation of electrodes and electrophysiological recording in vivo , the results showed that Rg1 restored the long - term potentiation ( LTP ) impaired by morphine in both freely moving and anaesthetised rats .

label: TextChunk
name: The electrophysiological recording in vitro showed that Rg1 restored the LTP in slices from the rats treated with morphine , but not changed LTP in the slices from normal saline - or morphine / Rg1 - treated rats ; this restoration could be inhibited by N-methyl-D-aspartate ( NMDA ) receptor antagonist MK801 .

label: TextChunk
name: We conclude that Rg1 may significantly improve the spatial learning capacity impaired by chonic morphine administration and restore the morphine - inhibited LTP .

name: A study on the effect of the duration of subcutaneous heparin injection on bruising and pain .

label: Disease
name: bruising

label: TextChunk
name: AIM : This study was carried out to determine the effect of injection duration on bruising and pain following the administration of the subcutaneous injection of heparin .

label: TextChunk
name: BACKGROUND : Although different methods to prevent bruising and pain following the subcutaneous injection of heparin have been widely studied and described , the effect of injection duration on the occurrence of bruising and pain is little documented .

label: TextChunk
name: DESIGN : This study was designed as within - subject , quasi - experimental research .

label: TextChunk
name: METHOD : The sample for the study consisted of 50 patients to whom subcutaneous heparin was administered .

label: TextChunk
name: Heparin was injected over 10 seconds on the right abdominal site and 30 seconds on the left abdominal site .

label: Chemical
name: Heparin

label: TextChunk
name: Injections areas were assessed for the presence of bruising at 48 and 72 hours after each injection .

label: TextChunk
name: Dimensions of the bruising on the heparin applied areas were measured using transparent millimetric measuring paper .

label: TextChunk
name: The visual analog scale ( VAS ) was used to measure pain intensity and a stop - watch was used to time the pain period .

label: TextChunk
name: Data were analysed using chi - square test , Mann - Whitney U , Wilcoxon signed ranks tests and correlation .

label: TextChunk
name: RESULTS : The percentage of bruising occurrence was 64 % with the injection of 10 seconds duration and 42 % in the 30 - second injection .

label: TextChunk
name: It was determined that the size of the bruising was smaller in the 30 - second injection .

label: TextChunk
name: Pain intensity and pain period were statistically significantly lower for the 30 - second injection than for the 10 - second injection .

label: TextChunk
name: CONCLUSIONS : It was determined that injection duration had an effect on bruising and pain following the subcutaneous administration of heparin .

label: TextChunk
name: This study should be repeated on a larger sample .

label: TextChunk
name: RELEVANCE TO CLINICAL PRACTICE : When administering subcutaneous heparin

name: Acute psychosis due to treatment with phenytoin in a nonepileptic patient .

name: Acute psychosis

label: TextChunk
name: The development of psychosis related to antiepileptic drug treatment is usually attributed to the interaction between the epileptic brain substratum and the antiepileptic drugs .

label: TextChunk
name: The case of a nonepileptic patient who developed psychosis following phenytoin treatment for trigeminal neuralgia is described .

label: Disease
name: trigeminal neuralgia

label: TextChunk
name: This case suggests that the psychotic symptoms that occur following phenytoin treatment in some epileptic patients may be the direct result of medication , unrelated to seizures

name: The effect of treatment with gum Arabic on gentamicin nephrotoxicity in rats : a preliminary study .

label: Chemical
name: gum Arabic

label: Disease
name: gentamicin nephrotoxicity

label: TextChunk
name: In the present work we assessed the effect of treatment of rats with gum Arabic on acute renal failure induced by gentamicin ( GM ) nephrotoxicity .

label: Chemical
name: GM

label: TextChunk
name: Rats were treated with the vehicle ( 2 mL / kg of distilled water and 5 % w / v cellulose , 10 days ), gum Arabic ( 2 mL / kg of a 10 % w / v aqueous suspension of gum Arabic powder , orally for 10 days ), or gum Arabic concomitantly with GM ( 80mg / kg / day intramuscularly , during the last six days of the treatment period ). Nephrotoxicity was assessed by measuring the concentrations of creatinine and urea in the plasma and reduced glutathione ( GSH ) in the kidney cortex , and by light microscopic examination of kidney sections .

label: TextChunk
name: The results indicated that concomitant treatment with gum Arabic and GM significantly increased creatinine and urea by about 183 and 239 %, respectively ( compared to 432 and 346 %, respectively , in rats treated with cellulose and GM ), and decreased that of cortical GSH by 21 %( compared to 27 % in the cellulose plus GM group ) The GM - induced proximal tubular necrosis appeared to be slightly less severe in rats given GM together with gum Arabic than in those given GM and cellulose .

label: Disease
name: tubular necrosis

label: TextChunk
name: It could be inferred that gum Arabic treatment has induced a modest amelioration of some of the histological and biochemical indices of GM nephrotoxicity .

label: Disease
name: GM nephrotoxicity

label: TextChunk
name: Further work is warranted on the effect of the treatments on renal functional aspects in models of chronic renal failure

label: Disease
name: Visual hallucinations

label: Chemical
name: zonisamide

label: TextChunk
name: Zonisamide is a broad - spectrum antiepileptic drug used to treat various types of seizures .

label: Chemical
name: Zonisamide

label: TextChunk
name: Although visual hallucinations have not been reported as an adverse effect of this agent , we describe three patients who experienced complex visual hallucinations and altered mental status after zonisamide treatment was begun or its dosage increased .

label: Disease
name: visual hallucinations

label: TextChunk
name: All three had been diagnosed earlier with epilepsy , and their electroencephalogram ( EEG ) findings were abnormal .

label: TextChunk
name: During monitoring , visual hallucinations did not correlate with EEG readings , nor did video recording capture any of the described events .

label: TextChunk
name: of the patients had experienced visual hallucinations before this event .

label: TextChunk
name: The only recent change in their treatment was the introduction or increased dosage of zonisamide .

label: TextChunk
name: With either discontinuation or decreased dosage of the drug the symptoms disappeared and did not recur .

label: TextChunk
name: Further observations and reports will help clarify this adverse effect .

label: TextChunk
name: Until then , clinicians need to be aware of this possible complication associated with zonisamide

name: Test conditions influence the response to a drug challenge in rodents .

label: TextChunk
name: These studies were conducted to examine the differential response to a drug challenge under varied experimental test conditions routinely employed to study drug - induced behavioral and neurophysiological responses in rodents .

label: TextChunk
name: Apomorphine , a nonselective dopamine agonist , was selected due to its biphasic behavioral effects , its ability to induce hypothermia , and to produce distinct changes to dopamine turnover in the rodent brain .

label: Chemical

name: dopamine agonist

label: TextChunk
name: From such experiments there is evidence that characterization and detection of apomorphine - induced activity in rodents critically depends upon the test conditions employed .

label: TextChunk
name: In rats , detection of apomorphine - induced hyperactivity was facilitated by a period of acclimatization to the test conditions .

label: TextChunk
name: Moreover , test conditions can impact upon other physiological responses to apomorphine such as drug - induced hypothermia .

label: TextChunk
name: In mice , apomorphine produced qualitatively different responses under novel conditions when compared to those behaviors elicited in the home test cage .

label: TextChunk
name: Drug - induced gross activity counts were increased in the novel exploratory box only , while measures of stereotypic behavior were similar in both .

label: TextChunk
name: By contrast , apomorphine - induced locomotion was more prominent in the novel exploratory box .

label: TextChunk
name: Dopamine turnover ratios ( DOPAC : DA and HVA : DA ) were found to be lower in those animals exposed to the exploratory box when compared to their home cage counterparts .
